pattern: flip() would be O(n) per call if it actually rewrote every bit, so instead track a single 'is the whole set currently inverted' flag and reinterpret every stored raw bit through it; only toString has to ever materialize the real bits.
hints: fix, unfix, all, one, and count all need to be fast even with up to 1e5 calls total, but flip toggles every single bit — if flip() literally rewrites the whole array every time, a long alternating sequence of flips is quadratic.

Store each bit's 'raw' value and a single global boolean meaning 'everything is currently inverted from its raw value'. The bit a caller actually observes at index i is raw[i] XOR that global flag. flip() then only has to toggle the one flag, O(1).

fix(idx) wants the OBSERVED bit to become 1, so it sets raw[idx] to (1 XOR flag); unfix(idx) wants it to become 0, so raw[idx] becomes (0 XOR flag). Track a running count of raw ones so count()/all()/one() can derive the observed count in O(1) via the flag (observed ones = flag ? size - rawOnes : rawOnes) instead of rescanning. Only toString must walk every index, since it has to produce the actual string.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: fix two bits, flip, check all(), unfix, flip again, check one(), unfix, count, then toString
size 1, the smallest allowed bitset, toggled through fix/unfix/flip
fixing an already-fixed bit, and unfixing an already-unfixed bit, must not change the count
flipping an all-zero bitset makes every bit 1, satisfying all() and one()
two flips in a row (a double negation) must return every bit exactly to its pre-flip state
fixing then unfixing the same bit after a flip must land on the correct raw value under the active flip flag
fixing every single bit individually, then checking all/one/count/toString all agree

stress.description: random fix/unfix/flip/all/one/count on a modest bitset, with a small, bounded number of toString calls scattered through
stress.note: toString is offered only occasionally and capped at 5 total calls to respect the constraint on how many times it may be invoked

complexity.time: O(1) per op except O(n) toString
